content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

May 2, 1991

Janice Rogers Brown  
Legal Affairs Secretary  
Office of the Governor  
State Capitol, First Floor  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-91-230

Dear Ms. Brown:

This is to confirm the advice I rendered to your office by telephone on April 9, 1991.

Specifically, I was asked whether an unsolicited magazine subscription was considered "informational material," and therefore not a gift to the Governor under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Section 82028(b)(1) states that for purposes of the Act, the term "gift" does not include "[i]nformational material such as books, reports, pamphlets, calendars or periodicals."

Since a magazine can provide as much, if not more, information relevant to governmental business as a book or periodical, Commission staff views magazines as "informational material" subject to the exception set forth in Section 82028(b)(1). Therefore, a magazine subscription provided to the Governor is not a gift under the Act and need not be reported in the Governor's Statement of Economic Interests.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.

Should you have further questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

SH:aa
